docs.title: Welcome to My Documentation Portal
docs.text:
This site is part of my journey towards becoming a Documentation Architect, combining:

  • DocOps (Documentation as Code)
  • Information Architecture
  • AI-assisted documentation workflows
  • CI/CD-driven documentation systems

Feel free to explore each section using the navigation bar.

docs.title: AI in Documentation
docs.text:
This section explores how AI can assist documentation workflows:

  • Automating rewrites and editing
  • Generating structured docs
  • Extracting metadata
  • Ensuring consistency
  • Detecting missing sections
  • Improving clarity and accuracy

docs.title: Information Architecture
docs.text:
Information Architecture (IA) is the structure behind effective documentation.

Key concepts:

  • Content modeling
  • Metadata
  • Taxonomy
  • Navigation
  • Chunking for LLMs
  • Findability & discoverability

docs.title: DocOps: CI/CD Pipeline
docs.text:
This site is deployed using:

  • GitHub Actions
  • MkDocs
  • GitHub Pages
  • Automated deployment to gh-pages branch
  • Documentation-as-Code practices

Whenever content is updated and pushed to the main branch:

  1. GitHub Actions creates a fresh Ubuntu VM
  2. Installs MkDocs
  3. Builds the site
  4. Pushes the generated HTML to the gh-pages branch
  5. GitHub Pages updates the live site Note: For GitHub pages to reflect the changes real time, Build and Deployment > Source will have to be set as 'Deploy From Branch' followed by gh-pages > '/root', this will have to be done first time only to activate pages afterwards set it back to GitHub Actions.

docs.title: Understanding the CI/CD Pipeline for Documentation
Overview
docs.text:
Documentation-as-Code (DocOps) treats technical documentation with the same rigor as software development. This page explains the complete CI/CD pipeline that powers this documentation portal — from commit to live deployment.

The workflow is powered by:

  • MkDocs for site generation
  • GitHub Actions for CI/CD
  • GitHub Pages for hosting
  • Automation for reliability and repeatability

docs.title: Why CI/CD Matters for Documentation
docs.text:
Traditionally, documentation is manually:

  • written
  • reviewed
  • exported
  • uploaded
  • deployed

This causes:

❌ inconsistencies ❌ outdated content ❌ manual errors ❌ deployment delays

DocOps solves this using automation.

Info

CI/CD brings consistency, speed, and scalability to documentation workflows.

docs.title: The High-Level Pipeline Flow
docs.text:
The documentation pipeline works as follows:

  1. You write or update .md files in the repo
  2. You commit and push changes to the main branch
  3. GitHub Actions automatically triggers a workflow
  4. A fresh Ubuntu VM is created
  5. MkDocs builds the static site
  6. The generated HTML is pushed to the gh-pages branch
  7. GitHub Pages serves the updated site publicly

✅ No manual uploads ✅ Automatic deployment ✅ Version-controlled documentation

docs.title: Trigger: When the Pipeline Starts
docs.text:
The pipeline starts automatically when a commit is pushed to main:

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

docs.title: Introduction & Overview
docs.text:
This section contains an overview of the purpose of this documentation portal.

Goals:

  • Build a professional, production-grade documentation system
  • Demonstrate DocOps workflows
  • Experiment with AI in documentation
  • Explore information architecture
  • Develop a strong portfolio for Documentation Architect roles

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Documentation Architecture is the discipline of designing, structuring, scaling, and governing documentation systems. It sits at the intersection of:

  • Information Architecture (IA)
  • Technical content strategy
  • Documentation-as-code (DocOps)
  • Developer experience (DX)
  • AI-enabled documentation workflows

Where Technical Writing focuses on producing content, Documentation Architecture focuses on designing the system that produces, delivers, and maintains content at scale.

docs.title: Why Documentation Architecture Matters
docs.text:
As products grow, documentation becomes:

  • harder to maintain
  • inconsistent across teams
  • resistant to change
  • technically outdated
  • fragmented across tools

Documentation Architecture solves these challenges by providing:

✅ A structured content model ✅ A unified taxonomy ✅ Consistent content patterns ✅ Automated publishing pipelines ✅ Governance practices ✅ Findable, navigable, high-quality docs

Note

Documentation Architecture is not writing — it is designing the ecosystem in which writing happens.

docs.title: Key Responsibilities of a Documentation Architect
📌 1. Define Content Strategy
docs.text:
• Information types (Concept, Task, Reference, etc.)
  • Documentation scope and boundaries
  • Audience modeling and persona mapping

docs.title: 📌 2. Structure Content (Information Architecture)
docs.text:
• Hierarchy and navigation
  • Content grouping
  • Metadata and taxonomy
  • Relationships between topics

Tip

Good IA ensures users find what they need in 3 clicks or less.

docs.title: 📌 3. Build Documentation Systems (DocOps)
docs.text:
• Version-controlled documentation
  • CI/CD–based publishing
  • Automated checks (linting, link tests, structure validation)
  • Deployment via GitHub Actions

docs.title: 📌 4. Enable Writers & Engineers
docs.text:
• Templates
  • Style guides
  • Component libraries
  • Reusable patterns

docs.title: 📌 5. Integrate AI into Documentation Workflows
docs.text:
• Automated summaries
  • Consistency checks
  • Metadata extraction
  • Semantic search
  • RAG-based documentation assistants

docs.title: Documentation Architect vs Technical Writer
docs.text:
Technical Writer Documentation Architect Writes content Designs documentation systems Works on assigned pages Designs structure across teams Follows style guide Creates style guide & templates Focus on clarity Focus on scalability, IA, and consistency Works inside the system Builds and owns the system

Info

A Documentation Architect is a technical strategist, not just a writer.

docs.title: When Do Companies Need a Documentation Architect?
docs.text:
Companies reach an inflection point:

  • Too many engineers writing inconsistent docs
  • Docs scattered across tools (Confluence, Notion, SharePoint, GitHub)
  • Documentation scaling beyond a single writer
  • Team needs automation, governance, and consistency
  • Developers rely heavily on docs for onboarding & feature adoption

This is when organizations move from:

❌ “Write documentation when needed” ➡️ to ✅ “Design a sustainable documentation system”

docs.title: What You Will Learn in This Portal
docs.text: This documentation portal demonstrates:

docs.title: ✅ Documentation Architecture
docs.text:
• IA patterns
  • Content modeling
  • Navigation design
  • Templates

docs.title: ✅ DocOps
docs.text:
• GitHub CI/CD pipelines
  • Documentation-as-Code
  • MkDocs + Material setup

docs.title: ✅ AI for Documentation
docs.text:
• How AI integrates with doc systems
  • Semantic search
  • LLM-assisted workflows

docs.title: ✅ Authoring Excellence
docs.text:
• Structured writing
  • Reusable content patterns
  • Consistency & clarity

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Documentation Architecture is the foundation of modern technical content. It enables:

  • scalability
  • consistency
  • automation
  • navigability
  • quality at scale

It transforms documentation from “pages” into a system.
